ESV.1: And the high priest said, “Are these things so?”
ESV.2: And Stephen said: “Brothers and fathers, hear me. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,
ESV.3: and said to him, ‘Go out from your land and from your kindred and go into the land that I will show you.’
ESV.4: Then he went out from the land of the Chaldeans and lived in Haran. And after his father died, God removed him from there into this land in which you are now living.
ESV.5: Yet he gave him no inheritance in it, not even a foot's length, but promised to give it to him as a possession and to his offspring after him, though he had no child.
ESV.6: And God spoke to this effect — that his offspring would be sojourners in a land belonging to others, who would enslave them and afflict them four hundred years.
ESV.7: ‘But I will judge the nation that they serve,’ said God, ‘and after that they shall come out and worship me in this place.’
ESV.8: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision. And so Abraham became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day, and Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.
ESV.9: “And the patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt; but God was with him
ESV.10: and rescued him out of all his afflictions and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who made him ruler over Egypt and over all his household.
ESV.11: Now there came a famine throughout all Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction, and our fathers could find no food.
ESV.12: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent out our fathers on their first visit.
ESV.13: And on the second visit Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph's family became known to Pharaoh.
ESV.14: And Joseph sent and summoned Jacob his father and all his kindred, seventy-five persons in all.
ESV.15: And Jacob went down into Egypt, and he died, he and our fathers,
ESV.16: and they were carried back to Shechem and laid in the tomb that Abraham had bought for a sum of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
ESV.17: “But as the time of the promise drew near, which God had granted to Abraham, the people increased and multiplied in Egypt
ESV.18: until there arose over Egypt another king who did not know Joseph.
ESV.19: He dealt shrewdly with our race and forced our fathers to expose their infants, so that they would not be kept alive.
ESV.20: At this time Moses was born; and he was beautiful in God's sight. And he was brought up for three months in his father's house,
ESV.21: and when he was exposed, Pharaoh's daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.
ESV.22: And Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and he was mighty in his words and deeds.
ESV.23: “When he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brothers, the children of Israel.
ESV.24: And seeing one of them being wronged, he defended the oppressed man and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian.
ESV.25: He supposed that his brothers would understand that God was giving them salvation by his hand, but they did not understand.
ESV.26: And on the following day he appeared to them as they were quarreling and tried to reconcile them, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why do you wrong each other?’
ESV.27: But the man who was wronging his neighbor thrust him aside, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?
ESV.28: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’
ESV.29: At this retort Moses fled and became an exile in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.
ESV.30: “Now when forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire in a bush.
ESV.31: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight, and as he drew near to look, there came the voice of the Lord:
ESV.32: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham and of Isaac and of Jacob.’ And Moses trembled and did not dare to look.
ESV.33: Then the Lord said to him, ‘Take off the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.
ESV.34: I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send you to Egypt.’
ESV.35: “This Moses, whom they rejected, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’ — this man God sent as both ruler and redeemer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
ESV.36: This man led them out, performing wonders and signs in Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the wilderness for forty years.
ESV.37: This is the Moses who said to the Israelites, ‘God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your brothers.’
ESV.38: This is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our fathers. He received living oracles to give to us.
ESV.39: Our fathers refused to obey him, but thrust him aside, and in their hearts they turned to Egypt,
ESV.40: saying to Aaron, ‘Make for us gods who will go before us. As for this Moses who led us out from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.’
ESV.41: And they made a calf in those days, and offered a sacrifice to the idol and were rejoicing in the works of their hands.
ESV.42: But God turned away and gave them over to worship the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: “‘Did you bring to me slain beasts and sacrifices, during the forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?
ESV.43: You took up the tent of Moloch and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made to worship; and I will send you into exile beyond Babylon.’
ESV.44: “Our fathers had the tent of witness in the wilderness, just as he who spoke to Moses directed him to make it, according to the pattern that he had seen.
ESV.45: Our fathers in turn brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations that God drove out before our fathers. So it was until the days of David,
ESV.46: who found favor in the sight of God and asked to find a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
ESV.47: But it was Solomon who built a house for him.
ESV.48: Yet the Most High does not dwell in houses made by hands, as the prophet says,
ESV.49: “‘Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or what is the place of my rest?
ESV.50: Did not my hand make all these things?’
ESV.51: “You stiff-necked people, uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit. As your fathers did, so do you.
ESV.52: Which of the prophets did your fathers not persecute? And they killed those who announced beforehand the coming of the Righteous One, whom you have now betrayed and murdered,
ESV.53: you who received the law as delivered by angels and did not keep it.”
ESV.54: Now when they heard these things they were enraged, and they ground their teeth at him.
ESV.55: But he, full of the Holy Spirit, gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.
ESV.56: And he said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.”
ESV.57: But they cried out with a loud voice and stopped their ears and rushed together  at him.
ESV.58: Then they cast him out of the city and stoned him. And the witnesses laid down their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.
ESV.59: And as they were stoning Stephen, he called out, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”
ESV.60: And falling to his knees he cried out with a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them.” And when he had said this, he fell asleep.

NASB.1: The high priest said, 'Are these things so?'
NASB.2: And he said, 'Hear me, brethren and fathers! The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,
NASB.3: and said to him, 'LEAVE YOUR COUNTRY AND YOUR RELATIVES, AND COME INTO THE LAND THAT I WILL SHOW YOU.'
NASB.4: 'Then he left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. From there, after his father died, [God] had him move to this country in which you are now living.
NASB.5: 'But He gave him no inheritance in it, not even a foot of ground, and [yet], even when he had no child, He promised that HE WOULD GIVE IT TO HIM AS A POSSESSION, AND TO HIS DESCENDANTS AFTER HIM.
NASB.6: 'But God spoke to this effect, that his DESCENDANTS WOULD BE ALIENS IN A FOREIGN LAND, AND THAT THEY WOULD BE ENSLAVED AND MISTREATED FOR FOUR HUNDRED YEARS.
NASB.7: ''AND WHATEVER NATION TO WHICH THEY WILL BE IN BONDAGE I MYSELF WILL JUDGE,' said God, 'AND AFTER THAT THEY WILL COME OUT AND SERVE ME IN THIS PLACE.'
NASB.8: 'And He gave him the covenant of circumcision; and so [Abraham] became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day; and Isaac [became the father of] Jacob, and Jacob [of] the twelve patriarchs.
NASB.9: 'The patriarchs became jealous of Joseph and sold him into Egypt. [Yet] God was with him,
NASB.10: and rescued him from all his afflictions, and granted him favor and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and he made him governor over Egypt and all his household.
NASB.11: 'Now a famine came over all Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction [with it], and our fathers could find no food.
NASB.12: 'But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our fathers [there] the first time.
NASB.13: 'On the second [visit] Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph's family was disclosed to Pharaoh.
NASB.14: 'Then Joseph sent [word] and invited Jacob his father and all his relatives to come to him, seventy-five persons [in all].
NASB.15: 'And Jacob went down to Egypt and [there] he and our fathers died.
NASB.16: '[From there] they were removed to Shechem and laid in the tomb which Abraham had purchased for a sum of money from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
NASB.17: 'But as the time of the promise was approaching which God had assured to Abraham, the people increased and multiplied in Egypt,
NASB.18: until THERE AROSE ANOTHER KING OVER EGYPT WHO KNEW NOTHING ABOUT JOSEPH.
NASB.19: 'It was he who took shrewd advantage of our race and mistreated our fathers so that they would expose their infants and they would not survive.
NASB.20: 'It was at this time that Moses was born; and he was lovely in the sight of God, and he was nurtured three months in his father's home.
NASB.21: 'And after he had been set outside, Pharaoh's daughter took him away and nurtured him as her own son.
NASB.22: 'Moses was educated in all the learning of the Egyptians, and he was a man of power in words and deeds.
NASB.23: 'But when he was approaching the age of forty, it entered his mind to visit his brethren, the sons of Israel.
NASB.24: 'And when he saw one [of them] being treated unjustly, he defended him and took vengeance for the oppressed by striking down the Egyptian.
NASB.25: 'And he supposed that his brethren understood that God was granting them deliverance through him, but they did not understand.
NASB.26: 'On the following day he appeared to them as they were fighting together, and he tried to reconcile them in peace, saying, 'Men, you are brethren, why do you injure one another?'
NASB.27: 'But the one who was injuring his neighbor pushed him away, saying, 'WHO MADE YOU A RULER AND JUDGE OVER US?
NASB.28: 'YOU DO NOT MEAN TO KILL ME AS YOU KILLED THE EGYPTIAN YESTERDAY, DO YOU?'
NASB.29: 'At this remark, MOSES FLED AND BECAME AN ALIEN IN THE LAND OF MIDIAN, where he became the father of two sons.
NASB.30: 'After forty years had passed, AN ANGEL APPEARED TO HIM IN THE WILDERNESS OF MOUNT Sinai, IN THE FLAME OF A BURNING THORN BUSH.
NASB.31: 'When Moses saw it, he marveled at the sight; and as he approached to look [more] closely, there came the voice of the Lord:
NASB.32: 'I AM THE GOD OF YOUR FATHERS, THE GOD OF ABRAHAM AND ISAAC AND JACOB.' Moses shook with fear and would not venture to look.
NASB.33: 'BUT THE LORD SAID TO HIM, 'TAKE OFF THE SANDALS FROM YOUR FEET, FOR THE PLACE ON WHICH YOU ARE STANDING IS HOLY GROUND.
NASB.34: 'I HAVE CERTAINLY SEEN THE OPPRESSION OF MY PEOPLE IN EGYPT AND HAVE HEARD THEIR GROANS, AND I HAVE COME DOWN TO RESCUE THEM; COME NOW, AND I WILL SEND YOU TO EGYPT.'
NASB.35: 'This Moses whom they disowned, saying, 'WHO MADE YOU A RULER AND A JUDGE?' is the one whom God sent [to be] both a ruler and a deliverer with the help of the angel who appeared to him in the thorn bush.
NASB.36: 'This man led them out, performing wonders and signs in the land of Egypt and in the Red Sea and in the wilderness for forty years.
NASB.37: 'This is the Moses who said to the sons of Israel, 'GOD WILL RAISE UP FOR YOU A PROPHET LIKE ME FROM YOUR BRETHREN.'
NASB.38: 'This is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness together with the angel who was speaking to him on Mount Sinai, and [who was] with our fathers; and he received living oracles to pass on to you.
NASB.39: 'Our fathers were unwilling to be obedient to him, but repudiated him and in their hearts turned back to Egypt,
NASB.40: SAYING TO AARON, 'MAKE FOR US GODS WHO WILL GO BEFORE US; FOR THIS MOSES WHO LED US OUT OF THE LAND OF EGYPT-- WE DO NOT KNOW WHAT HAPPENED TO HIM.'
NASB.41: 'At that time they made a calf and brought a sacrifice to the idol, and were rejoicing in the works of their hands.
NASB.42: 'But God turned away and delivered them up to serve the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, 'IT WAS NOT TO ME THAT YOU OFFERED VICTIMS AND SACRIFICES FORTY YEARS IN THE WILDERNESS, WAS IT, O HOUSE OF ISRAEL?
NASB.43: 'YOU ALSO TOOK ALONG THE TABERNACLE OF MOLOCH AND THE STAR OF THE GOD ROMPHA, THE IMAGES WHICH YOU MADE TO WORSHIP. I ALSO WILL REMOVE YOU BEYOND BABYLON.'
NASB.44: 'Our fathers had the tabernacle of testimony in the wilderness, just as He who spoke to Moses directed [him] to make it according to the pattern which he had seen.
NASB.45: 'And having received it in their turn, our fathers brought it in with Joshua upon dispossessing the nations whom God drove out before our fathers, until the time of David.
NASB.46: '[David] found favor in God's sight, and asked that he might find a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
NASB.47: 'But it was Solomon who built a house for Him.
NASB.48: 'However, the Most High does not dwell in [houses] made by [human] hands; as the prophet says:
NASB.49: 'HEAVEN IS MY THRONE, AND EARTH IS THE FOOTSTOOL OF MY FEET; WHAT KIND OF HOUSE WILL YOU BUILD FOR ME?' says the Lord, 'OR WHAT PLACE IS THERE FOR MY REPOSE?
NASB.50: 'WAS IT NOT MY HAND WHICH MADE ALL THESE THINGS?'
NASB.51: 'You men who are stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart and ears are always resisting the Holy Spirit; you are doing just as your fathers did.
NASB.52: 'Which one of the prophets did your fathers not persecute? They killed those who had previously announced the coming of the Righteous One, whose betrayers and murderers you have now become;
NASB.53: you who received the law as ordained by angels, and [yet] did not keep it.'
NASB.54: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the quick, and they [began] gnashing their teeth at him.
NASB.55: But being full of the Holy Spirit, he gazed intently into heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God;
NASB.56: and he said, 'Behold, I see the heavens opened up and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.'
NASB.57: But they cried out with a loud voice, and covered their ears and rushed at him with one impulse.
NASB.58: When they had driven him out of the city, they [began] stoning [him]; and the witnesses laid aside their robes at the feet of a young man named Saul.
NASB.59: They went on stoning Stephen as he called on [the Lord] and said, 'Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!'
NASB.60: Then falling on his knees, he cried out with a loud voice, 'Lord, do not hold this sin against them!' Having said this, he fell asleep.

NLT.1: Then the high priest asked Stephen, “Are these accusations true?”
NLT.2: This was Stephen’s reply: “Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham in Mesopotamia before he settled in Haran.
NLT.3: God told him, ‘Leave your native land and your relatives, and come into the land that I will show you.’
NLT.4: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and lived in Haran until his father died. Then God brought him here to the land where you now live.
NLT.5: “But God gave him no inheritance here, not even one square foot of land. God did promise, however, that eventually the whole land would belong to Abraham and his descendants — even though he had no children yet.
NLT.6: God also told him that his descendants would live in a foreign land, where they would be oppressed as slaves for 400 years.
NLT.7: ‘But I will punish the nation that enslaves them,’ God said, ‘and in the end they will come out and worship me here in this place.’
NLT.8: “God also gave Abraham the covenant of circumcision at that time. So when Abraham became the father of Isaac, he circumcised him on the eighth day. And the practice was continued when Isaac became the father of Jacob, and when Jacob became the father of the twelve patriarchs of the Israelite nation.
NLT.9: “These patriarchs were jealous of their brother Joseph, and they sold him to be a slave in Egypt. But God was with him
NLT.10: and rescued him from all his troubles. And God gave him favor before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. God also gave Joseph unusual wisdom, so that Pharaoh appointed him governor over all of Egypt and put him in charge of the palace.
NLT.11: “But a famine came upon Egypt and Canaan. There was great misery, and our ancestors ran out of food.
NLT.12: Jacob heard that there was still grain in Egypt, so he sent his sons — our ancestors — to buy some.
NLT.13: The second time they went, Joseph revealed his identity to his brothers, and they were introduced to Pharaoh.
NLT.14: Then Joseph sent for his father, Jacob, and all his relatives to come to Egypt, seventy-five persons in all.
NLT.15: So Jacob went to Egypt. He died there, as did our ancestors.
NLT.16: Their bodies were taken to Shechem and buried in the tomb Abraham had bought for a certain price from Hamor’s sons in Shechem.
NLT.17: “As the time drew near when God would fulfill his promise to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt greatly increased.
NLT.18: But then a new king came to the throne of Egypt who knew nothing about Joseph.
NLT.19: This king exploited our people and oppressed them, forcing parents to abandon their newborn babies so they would die.
NLT.20: “At that time Moses was born — a beautiful child in God’s eyes. His parents cared for him at home for three months.
NLT.21: When they had to abandon him, Pharaoh’s daughter adopted him and raised him as her own son.
NLT.22: Moses was taught all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and he was powerful in both speech and action.
NLT.23: “One day when Moses was forty years old, he decided to visit his relatives, the people of Israel.
NLT.24: He saw an Egyptian mistreating an Israelite. So Moses came to the man’s defense and avenged him, killing the Egyptian.
NLT.25: Moses assumed his fellow Israelites would realize that God had sent him to rescue them, but they didn’t.
NLT.26: “The next day he visited them again and saw two men of Israel fighting. He tried to be a peacemaker. ‘Men,’ he said, ‘you are brothers. Why are you fighting each other?’
NLT.27: “But the man in the wrong pushed Moses aside. ‘Who made you a ruler and judge over us?’ he asked.
NLT.28: ‘Are you going to kill me as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?’
NLT.29: When Moses heard that, he fled the country and lived as a foreigner in the land of Midian. There his two sons were born.
NLT.30: “Forty years later, in the desert near Mount Sinai, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush.
NLT.31: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight. As he went to take a closer look, the voice of the LORD called out to him,
NLT.32: ‘I am the God of your ancestors — the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.’ Moses shook with terror and did not dare to look.
NLT.33: “Then the LORD said to him, ‘Take off your sandals, for you are standing on holy ground.
NLT.34: I have certainly seen the oppression of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now go, for I am sending you back to Egypt.’
NLT.35: “So God sent back the same man his people had previously rejected when they demanded, ‘Who made you a ruler and judge over us?’ Through the angel who appeared to him in the burning bush, God sent Moses to be their ruler and savior.
NLT.36: And by means of many wonders and miraculous signs, he led them out of Egypt, through the Red Sea, and through the wilderness for forty years.
NLT.37: “Moses himself told the people of Israel, ‘God will raise up for you a Prophet like me from among your own people.’
NLT.38: Moses was with our ancestors, the assembly of God’s people in the wilderness, when the angel spoke to him at Mount Sinai. And there Moses received life-giving words to pass on to us.
NLT.39: “But our ancestors refused to listen to Moses. They rejected him and wanted to return to Egypt.
NLT.40: They told Aaron, ‘Make us some gods who can lead us, for we don’t know what has become of this Moses, who brought us out of Egypt.’
NLT.41: So they made an idol shaped like a calf, and they sacrificed to it and celebrated over this thing they had made.
NLT.42:
Then God turned away from them and abandoned them to serve the stars of heaven as their gods! In the book of the prophets it is written, ‘Was it to me you were bringing sacrifices and offerings
during those forty years in the wilderness, Israel?
NLT.43:
No, you carried your pagan gods —
the shrine of Molech,
the star of your god Rephan,
and the images you made to worship them.
So I will send you into exile
as far away as Babylon.’
NLT.44: “Our ancestors carried the Tabernacle with them through the wilderness. It was constructed according to the plan God had shown to Moses.
NLT.45: Years later, when Joshua led our ancestors in battle against the nations that God drove out of this land, the Tabernacle was taken with them into their new territory. And it stayed there until the time of King David.
NLT.46: “David found favor with God and asked for the privilege of building a permanent Temple for the God of Jacob.
NLT.47: But it was Solomon who actually built it.
NLT.48: However, the Most High doesn’t live in temples made by human hands. As the prophet says,
NLT.49:
‘Heaven is my throne,
and the earth is my footstool.
Could you build me a temple as good as that?’
asks the LORD.
‘Could you build me such a resting place?
NLT.50: Didn’t my hands make both heaven and earth?’
NLT.51: “You stubborn people! You are heathen at heart and deaf to the truth. Must you forever resist the Holy Spirit? That’s what your ancestors did, and so do you!
NLT.52: Name one prophet your ancestors didn’t persecute! They even killed the ones who predicted the coming of the Righteous One — the Messiah whom you betrayed and murdered.
NLT.53: You deliberately disobeyed God’s law, even though you received it from the hands of angels.”
NLT.54: The Jewish leaders were infuriated by Stephen’s accusation, and they shook their fists at him in rage.
NLT.55: But Stephen, full of the Holy Spirit, gazed steadily into heaven and saw the glory of God, and he saw Jesus standing in the place of honor at God’s right hand.
NLT.56: And he told them, “Look, I see the heavens opened and the Son of Man standing in the place of honor at God’s right hand!”
NLT.57: Then they put their hands over their ears and began shouting. They rushed at him
NLT.58: and dragged him out of the city and began to stone him. His accusers took off their coats and laid them at the feet of a young man named Saul.
NLT.59: As they stoned him, Stephen prayed, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”
NLT.60: He fell to his knees, shouting, “Lord, don’t charge them with this sin!” And with that, he died.

WEB.1: The high priest said, “Are these things so?”
WEB.2: He said, “Brothers and fathers, listen. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,
WEB.3: and said to him, ‘Get out of your land, and from your relatives, and come into a land which I will show you.’
WEB.4: Then he came out of the land of the Chaldaeans, and lived in Haran. From there, when his father was dead, God moved him into this land, where you are now living.
WEB.5: He gave him no inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on. He promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his offspring  after him, when he still had no child.
WEB.6: God spoke in this way: that his offspring  would live as aliens in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years.
WEB.7: ‘I will judge the nation to which they will be in bondage,’ said God, ‘and after that will they come out, and serve me in this place.’
WEB.8: He gave him the covenant of circumcision. So Abraham became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day. Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob became the father of the twelve patriarchs.
WEB.9: “The patriarchs, moved with jealousy against Joseph, sold him into Egypt. God was with him,
WEB.10: and delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. He made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
WEB.11: Now a famine came over all the land of Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction. Our fathers found no food.
WEB.12: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent out our fathers the first time.
WEB.13: On the second time Joseph was made known to his brothers, and Joseph’s race was revealed to Pharaoh.
WEB.14: Joseph sent, and summoned Jacob, his father, and all his relatives, seventy-five souls.
WEB.15: Jacob went down into Egypt, and he died, himself and our fathers,
WEB.16: and they were brought back to Shechem, and laid in the tomb that Abraham bought for a price in silver from the children of Hamor of Shechem.
WEB.17: “But as the time of the promise came close which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
WEB.18: until there arose a different king, who didn’t know Joseph.
WEB.19: The same took advantage of our race, and mistreated our fathers, and forced them to throw out their babies, so that they wouldn’t stay alive.
WEB.20: At that time Moses was born, and was exceedingly handsome. He was nourished three months in his father’s house.
WEB.21: When he was thrown out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and reared him as her own son.
WEB.22: Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians. He was mighty in his words and works.
WEB.23: But when he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brothers,  the children of Israel.
WEB.24: Seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him who was oppressed, striking the Egyptian.
WEB.25: He supposed that his brothers understood that God, by his hand, was giving them deliverance; but they didn’t understand.
WEB.26: “The day following, he appeared to them as they fought, and urged them to be at peace again, saying, ‘Sirs, you are brothers. Why do you wrong one another?’
WEB.27: But he who did his neighbor wrong pushed him away, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?
WEB.28: Do you want to kill me, as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’
WEB.29: Moses fled at this saying, and became a stranger in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.
WEB.30: “When forty years were fulfilled, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire in a bush.
WEB.31: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight. As he came close to see, a voice of the Lord came to him,
WEB.32: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’  Moses trembled, and dared not look.
WEB.33: The Lord said to him, ‘Take your sandals off of your feet, for the place where you stand is holy ground.
WEB.34: I have surely seen the affliction of my people that is in Egypt, and have heard their groaning. I have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you into Egypt.’
WEB.35: “This Moses, whom they refused, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’ — God has sent him as both a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
WEB.36: This man led them out, having worked wonders and signs in Egypt, in the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.
WEB.37: This is that Moses, who said to the children of Israel, ‘The Lord our God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me.’
WEB.38: This is he who was in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel that spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers, who received living revelations to give to us,
WEB.39: to whom our fathers wouldn’t be obedient, but rejected him, and turned back in their hearts to Egypt,
WEB.40: saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods that will go before us, for as for this Moses, who led us out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what has become of him.’
WEB.41: They made a calf in those days, and brought a sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their hands.
WEB.42: But God turned, and gave them up to serve the army of the sky,  as it is written in the book of the prophets, ‘Did you offer to me slain animals and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?
WEB.43: You took up the tabernacle of Moloch, the star of your god Rephan, the figures which you made to worship. I will carry you away  beyond Babylon.’
WEB.44: “Our fathers had the tabernacle of the testimony in the wilderness, even as he who spoke to Moses commanded him to make it according to the pattern that he had seen;
WEB.45: which also our fathers, in their turn, brought in with Joshua when they entered into the possession of the nations, whom God drove out before the face of our fathers, to the days of David,
WEB.46: who found favor in the sight of God, and asked to find a habitation for the God of Jacob.
WEB.47: But Solomon built him a house.
WEB.48: However, the Most High doesn’t dwell in temples made with hands, as the prophet says,
WEB.49: ‘heaven is my throne, and the earth a footstool for my feet. What kind of house will you build me?’ says the Lord; ‘or what is the place of my rest?
WEB.50: Didn’t my hand make all these things?’
WEB.51: “You stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit! As your fathers did, so you do.
WEB.52: Which of the prophets didn’t your fathers persecute? They killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One, of whom you have now become betrayers and murderers.
WEB.53: You received the law as it was ordained by angels, and didn’t keep it!”
WEB.54: Now when they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed at him with their teeth.
WEB.55: But he, being full of the Holy Spirit, looked up steadfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,
WEB.56: and said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”
WEB.57: But they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and rushed at him with one accord.
WEB.58: They threw him out of the city, and stoned him. The witnesses placed their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.
WEB.59: They stoned Stephen as he called out, saying, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!”
WEB.60: He kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, “Lord, don’t hold this sin against them!” When he had said this, he fell asleep.

AMP.1: Now the high priest asked [Stephen], "Are these charges true?"
AMP.2: And he answered, "Brothers and fathers, listen to me! The God of glory [the Shekinah, the radiance of God] appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,
AMP.3: and He said to him, '<e>Leave your country and your relatives, and come to the land that I will show you</e>.'
AMP.4: Then he left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. And from there, after his father died, God sent him to this country in which you now live.
AMP.5: But He did not give him inheritable property, not even enough ground to take a step on, yet He promised that <e>He would give it to Him as a possession, and to his descendants after him</e>.
AMP.6: And this is, in effect, what God spoke [to him]: That his descendants would be aliens (strangers) in a foreign land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years.
AMP.7: 'And I will judge any nation to whom they will be in bondage,' said God, 'and after that they will come out and serve Me [in worship] in this place.'
AMP.8: And God gave Abraham a covenant [a formal agreement to be strictly observed] of [which] circumcision [was the sign]; and so [under these circumstances] Abraham became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day; and Isaac [became the father] of Jacob, and Jacob [became the father] of the twelve patriarchs.
AMP.9: "The [ten elder] patriarchs, overwhelmed with jealousy, sold [their younger brother] Joseph into [slavery in] Egypt; but God was with him,
AMP.10: and He rescued him from all his suffering, and gave him favor and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and he made Joseph governor over Egypt and over his entire household.
AMP.11: "Now a famine came over all Egypt and Canaan, bringing great distress and our fathers could not find food [for their households and livestock].
AMP.12: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our fathers there the first time.
AMP.13: And on the second visit Joseph identified himself to his brothers, and Joseph's family and background were revealed to Pharaoh.
AMP.14: Then Joseph sent and invited Jacob his father and all his relatives to come to him, seventy-five persons in all.
AMP.15: And Jacob (Israel) went down into Egypt, and there he died, as did our fathers;
AMP.16: and [from Egypt] their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb which Abraham had purchased for a sum of money from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
AMP.17: "But as the time [for the fulfillment] of the promise which God had made to Abraham was approaching, the [Hebrew] people increased and multiplied in Egypt,
AMP.18: until [the time when] there arose another king over egypt who did not know joseph [nor his history and the merit of his service to Egypt].
AMP.19: He shrewdly exploited our race and mistreated our fathers, forcing them to expose their [male] babies so that they would die.
AMP.20: It was at this [critical] time that Moses was born; and he was lovely in the sight of God, and for three months he was nourished in his father's house.
AMP.21: Then when he was set outside [to die], Pharaoh's daughter rescued him and claimed him for herself, and cared for him as her own son.
AMP.22: So Moses was educated in all the wisdom and culture of the Egyptians, and he was a man of power in words and deeds.
AMP.23: But when he reached the age of forty, it came into his heart to visit his brothers, the sons of Israel.
AMP.24: And when he saw one [of them] being treated unfairly, he defended the oppressed man and avenged him by striking and killing the Egyptian.
AMP.25: He expected his countrymen to understand that God was granting them freedom through him [assuming that they would accept him], but they did not understand.
AMP.26: Then on the next day he suddenly appeared to two of them as they were fighting, and he tried to reconcile them, saying, 'Men, you are brothers; why do you wrong one another?'
AMP.27: But the man who was injuring his neighbor pushed Moses away, saying, '<e>Who appointed you ruler and judge over us</e>?
AMP.28: <e>Do you intend to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday</e>?'
AMP.29: At this remark <e>Moses fled and became an exile in the land of Midian</e>, where he fathered two sons.
AMP.30: "After forty years had passed, <e>an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount</e> Sinai, <e>in the flame of a burning thorn bush</e>.
AMP.31: When Moses saw it, he was astonished at the sight; but as he went near to look more closely, the voice of the Lord came [to him, saying]:
AMP.32: '<e>I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham and of Isaac and of Jacob</e>.' Moses trembled with fear and did not dare to look.
AMP.33: Then the Lord said to him, 'Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground [worthy of reverence and respect].
AMP.34: I have most certainly seen the oppression of My people in Egypt and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to rescue them. Now come, and I will send you to Egypt [as My messenger].'
AMP.35: "This Moses whom they rejected, saying, 'Who made you a ruler and a judge?' is the very one whom God sent to be both a ruler and a deliverer, with the [protecting and helping] hand of the Angel who appeared to him in the thorn bush.
AMP.36: This man led them out [of Egypt] after performing wonders and signs in the land of Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the wilderness for forty years.
AMP.37: This is the Moses who said to the children of Israel, '<e>God will raise up for you a Prophet</e><e>like me from your countrymen</e>.'
AMP.38: This is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness together with the Angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and who was with our fathers; and he received living oracles [divine words that still live] to be handed down to you.
AMP.39: Our fathers were unwilling to be subject to him [and refused to listen to him]. They rejected him, and in their hearts turned back to Egypt.
AMP.40: <e>They said to Aaron, 'Make for us gods who will go before us; for this Moses who led us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him</e>.'
AMP.41: In those days they made a calf and brought a sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced and celebrated over the works of their hands.
AMP.42: But God turned away [from them] and handed them over to serve the host of heaven. As it is written and forever remains written in the book of the prophets, 'It was not [really] to Me that you offered victims and sacrifices for forty years in the wilderness, was it, O house of Israel?
AMP.43: You also took along the tabernacle (portable temple) of Moloch and the star of the god Rompha, the images which you made to worship; and I will remove you beyond Babylon [carrying you away into exile].'
AMP.44: "Our fathers had the tabernacle of testimony in the wilderness, just as God directed Moses to make it according to the pattern which he had seen.
AMP.45: Our fathers also brought it in [with them into the land] with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations whom God drove out before our fathers, [and so it remained here] until the time of David,
AMP.46: who found favor (grace, spiritual blessing) in the sight of God and asked that he might [be allowed to] find a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
AMP.47: But it was Solomon who built a house for Him.
AMP.48: However, the Most High [the One infinitely exalted above humanity] does not dwell in houses made by human hands; as the prophet [Isaiah] says,
AMP.49:
'Heaven is My throne,
And the earth is the footstool for My feet;
What kind of house will you build for Me?' says the Lord,
'Or what place is there for My rest?
AMP.50: 'Was it not My hand that made all these things?'
AMP.51: "You stiff-necked and stubborn people, uncircumcised in heart and ears, you are always actively resisting the Holy Spirit. You are doing just as your fathers did.
AMP.52: Which one of the prophets did your fathers not persecute? They killed those who proclaimed beforehand the coming of the Righteous One, whose betrayers and murderers you have now become;
AMP.53: you who received the law as ordained and delivered to you by angels, and yet you did not obey it!"
AMP.54: Now when they heard this [accusation and understood its implication], they were cut to the heart, and they began grinding their teeth [in rage] at him.
AMP.55: But he, being full of the Holy Spirit and led by Him, gazed into heaven and saw the glory [the great splendor and majesty] of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God;
AMP.56: and he said, "Look! I see the heavens opened up [in welcome] and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!"
AMP.57: But they shouted with loud voices, and covered their ears and together rushed at him [considering him guilty of blasphemy].
AMP.58: Then they drove him out of the city and began stoning him; and the witnesses placed their outer robes at the feet of a young man named Saul.
AMP.59: They continued stoning Stephen as he called on the Lord and said, "Lord Jesus, receive and accept and welcome my spirit!"
AMP.60: Then falling on his knees [in worship], he cried out loudly, "Lord, do not hold this sin against them [do not charge them]!" When he had said this, he fell asleep [in death].

MSG.1: Then the Chief Priest said, "What do you have to say for yourself?"
MSG.2: Stephen replied, "Friends, fathers, and brothers, the God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was still in Mesopotamia, before the move to Haran,
MSG.3: and told him, 'Leave your country and family and go to the land I'll show you.'
MSG.4: "So he left the country of the Chaldees and moved to Haran. After the death of his father, he immigrated to this country where you now live,
MSG.5: but God gave him nothing, not so much as a foothold. He did promise to give the country to him and his son later on, even though Abraham had no son at the time.
MSG.6: God let him know that his offspring would move to an alien country where they would be enslaved and brutalized for four hundred years.
MSG.7: 'But,' God said, 'I will step in and take care of those slaveholders and bring my people out so they can worship me in this place.'
MSG.8: "Then he made a covenant with him and signed it in Abraham's flesh by circumcision. When Abraham had his son Isaac, within eight days he reproduced the sign of circumcision in him. Isaac became father of Jacob, and Jacob father of twelve 'fathers,' each faithfully passing on the covenant sign.
MSG.9: "But then those 'fathers,' burning up with jealousy, sent Joseph off to Egypt as a slave. God was right there with him, though—
MSG.10: he not only rescued him from all his troubles but brought him to the attention of Pharaoh, king of Egypt. He was so impressed with Joseph that he put him in charge of the whole country, including his own personal affairs.
MSG.11: "Later a famine descended on that entire region, stretching from Egypt to Canaan, bringing terrific hardship. Our hungry fathers looked high and low for food, but the cupboard was bare.
MSG.12: Jacob heard there was food in Egypt and sent our fathers to scout it out.
MSG.13: Having confirmed the report, they went back to Egypt a second time to get food. On that visit, Joseph revealed his true identity to his brothers and introduced the Jacob family to Pharaoh.
MSG.14: Then Joseph sent for his father, Jacob, and everyone else in the family, seventy-five in all.
MSG.15: That's how the Jacob family got to Egypt. "Jacob died, and our fathers after him.
MSG.16: They were taken to Shechem and buried in the tomb for which Abraham paid a good price to the sons of Hamor.
MSG.17: "When the four hundred years were nearly up, the time God promised Abraham for deliverance, the population of our people in Egypt had become very large.
MSG.18: And there was now a king over Egypt who had never heard of Joseph.
MSG.19: He exploited our race mercilessly. He went so far as forcing us to abandon our newborn infants, exposing them to the elements to die a cruel death.
MSG.20: "In just such a time Moses was born, a most beautiful baby. He was hidden at home for three months.
MSG.21: When he could be hidden no longer, he was put outside—and immediately rescued by Pharaoh's daughter, who mothered him as her own son.
MSG.22: Moses was educated in the best schools in Egypt. He was equally impressive as a thinker and an athlete.
MSG.23: "When he was forty years old, he wondered how everything was going with his Hebrew kin and went out to look things over.
MSG.24: He saw an Egyptian abusing one of them and stepped in, avenging his underdog brother by knocking the Egyptian flat.
MSG.25: He thought his brothers would be glad that he was on their side, and even see him as an instrument of God to deliver them. But they didn't see it that way.
MSG.26: The next day two of them were fighting and he tried to break it up, told them to shake hands and get along with each other: 'Friends, you are brothers, why are you beating up on each other?'
MSG.27: "The one who had started the fight said, 'Who put you in charge of us?
MSG.28: Are you going to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday?'
MSG.29: When Moses heard that, realizing that the word was out, he ran for his life and lived in exile over in Midian. During the years of exile, two sons were born to him.
MSG.30: "Forty years later, in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, an angel appeared to him in the guise of flames of a burning bush.
MSG.31: Moses, not believing his eyes, went up to take a closer look. He heard God's voice:
MSG.32: 'I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.' Frightened nearly out of his skin, Moses shut his eyes and turned away.
MSG.33: "God said, 'Kneel and pray. You are in a holy place, on holy ground.
MSG.34: I've seen the agony of my people in Egypt. I've heard their groans. I've come to help them. So get yourself ready; I'm sending you back to Egypt.'
MSG.35: "This is the same Moses whom they earlier rejected, saying, 'Who put you in charge of us?' This is the Moses that God, using the angel flaming in the burning bush, sent back as ruler and redeemer.
MSG.36: He led them out of their slavery. He did wonderful things, setting up God-signs all through Egypt, down at the Red Sea, and out in the wilderness for forty years.
MSG.37: This is the Moses who said to his congregation, 'God will raise up a prophet just like me from your descendants.'
MSG.38: This is the Moses who stood between the angel speaking at Sinai and your fathers assembled in the wilderness and took the life-giving words given to him and handed them over to us,
MSG.39: words our fathers would have nothing to do with. "They craved the old Egyptian ways,
MSG.40: whining to Aaron, 'Make us gods we can see and follow. This Moses who got us out here miles from nowhere—who knows what's happened to him!'
MSG.41: That was the time when they made a calf-idol, brought sacrifices to it, and congratulated each other on the wonderful religious program they had put together.
MSG.42: "God wasn't at all pleased; but he let them do it their way, worship every new god that came down the pike—and live with the consequences, consequences described by the prophet Amos: Did you bring me offerings of animals and grains those forty wilderness years, O Israel?
MSG.43: Hardly. You were too busy building shrines to war gods, to sex goddesses, Worshiping them with all your might. That's why I put you in exile in Babylon.
MSG.44: "And all this time our ancestors had a tent shrine for true worship, made to the exact specifications God provided Moses.
MSG.45: They had it with them as they followed Joshua, when God cleared the land of pagans, and still had it right down to the time of David.
MSG.46: David asked God for a permanent place for worship.
MSG.48: "Yet that doesn't mean that Most High God lives in a building made by carpenters and masons. The prophet Isaiah put it well when he wrote,
MSG.49: "Heaven is my throne room; I rest my feet on earth. So what kind of house will you build me?" says God. "Where I can get away and relax?
MSG.50: It's already built, and I built it."
MSG.51: "And you continue, so bullheaded! Calluses on your hearts, flaps on your ears! Deliberately ignoring the Holy Spirit, you're just like your ancestors.
MSG.52: Was there ever a prophet who didn't get the same treatment? Your ancestors killed anyone who dared talk about the coming of the Just One. And you've kept up the family tradition—traitors and murderers, all of you.
MSG.53: You had God's Law handed to you by angels—gift-wrapped!—and you squandered it!"
MSG.54: At that point they went wild, a rioting mob of catcalls and whistles and invective.
MSG.55: But Stephen, full of the Holy Spirit, hardly noticed—he only had eyes for God, whom he saw in all his glory with Jesus standing at his side.
MSG.56: He said, "Oh! I see heaven wide open and the Son of Man standing at God's side!"
MSG.57: Yelling and hissing, the mob drowned him out. Now in full stampede,
MSG.58: they dragged him out of town and pelted him with rocks. The ringleaders took off their coats and asked a young man named Saul to watch them.
MSG.59: As the rocks rained down, Stephen prayed, "Master Jesus, take my life."
MSG.60: Then he knelt down, praying loud enough for everyone to hear, "Master, don't blame them for this sin"—his last words. Then he died. Saul was right there, congratulating the killers.
